chief_complaint: A 32-year-old man presented with pain in the left maxillary region, fever spikes, photophobia, and horizontal diplopia.
history: The patient had a history of recent molar extraction, frequent alcohol intake, and cigarette smoking. He presented with symptoms of meningitis, including pain in the left maxillary region, fever spikes, photophobia, and horizontal diplopia.
exam: Examination revealed left sixth cranial nerve palsy and positive jolt accentuation. Vital signs were not mentioned.
labs: Laboratory tests revealed a systemic inflammatory response, and lumbar puncture confirmed neutrophilic meningitis with Fusobacterium nucleatum isolation. Magnetic resonance imaging revealed left cavernous sinus thrombosis.
correct_diagnosis: Fusobacterium nucleatum meningitis complicated with cavernous sinus thrombosis
differentials: Fusobacterium nucleatum meningitis complicated with cavernous sinus thrombosis
correct_ranking: Fusobacterium nucleatum meningitis complicated with cavernous sinus thrombosis
